name: frontend-aesthetics
description: Use when creating UI designs to avoid generic "AI slop" aesthetics. Enforces distinctive typography, cohesive color systems, purposeful motion, and atmospheric backgrounds. Critical for landing pages, dashboards, and branded interfaces.
capabilities: Guide font selection with distinctive typography alternatives beyond Inter/Roboto
Implement cohesive color systems with single accent color and true dark backgrounds
Add purposeful motion using CSS transitions and Framer Motion patterns
Create atmospheric depth with gradients, orbs, and layered backgrounds
Identify and avoid common AI-generated design anti-patterns
author: Barnhardt-Enterprises-Inc
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing design guidelines and code examples. Static scanner flagged 209 false positives: 121 markdown code block delimiters misidentified as Ruby backtick execution, 80 CSS patterns misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms, and 18 UI design terms misidentified as system reconnaissance. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or external commands present.
valueStatement: AI often defaults to generic, overused design patterns that make interfaces immediately recognizable as machine-generated. This skill provides specific alternatives for typography, color, motion, and depth to create professional, branded interfaces.
limitations: Does not generate actual code files or modify existing projects
Does not install or configure design system dependencies
Does not validate designs against accessibility standards
Requires manual implementation of suggested patterns

useCases.title: Professional Dashboard UI
useCases.description: Build data-dense dashboards with proper typography hierarchy, brand accent colors, and subtle motion for state changes.

useCases.title: Branded Landing Pages
useCases.description: Create landing pages with distinctive visual identity, atmospheric backgrounds, and purposeful micro-interactions.

useCases.title: Cohesive Design Systems
useCases.description: Establish consistent design tokens, component patterns, and motion standards across your application.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Design Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the frontend-aesthetics skill to design a button component with proper hover states, shadow effects, and consistent brand styling.

promptTemplates.title: Dashboard Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply frontend-aesthetics principles to create a professional dashboard card showing system metrics with proper typography, accent colors, and subtle animations.

promptTemplates.title: Landing Page
promptTemplates.prompt: Following frontend-aesthetics guidelines, design a landing page hero section with atmospheric depth, distinctive typography, and staggered reveal animations.

promptTemplates.title: Design Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the following UI code using frontend-aesthetics anti-patterns. Identify all violations and suggest specific improvements with corrected code examples.

bestPractices: Pick one brand accent color and use it consistently across all interactive elements
Use true dark backgrounds (neutral-950) instead of gray variants
Apply staggered reveals for lists using animation-delay with 100ms increments
Test reduced-motion preferences for accessibility compliance
antiPatterns: Using Inter/Roboto as primary fonts creates generic, AI-generated appearance
Purple/blue gradients and gray backgrounds signal AI-generated design
Static pages without any motion feel lifeless and unprofessional
Multiple primary buttons or colorful icons break visual hierarchy

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for frontend design guidance.

faq.question: What font families are recommended?
faq.answer: Plus Jakarta Sans, Bricolage Grotesque, Space Grotesk, and JetBrains Mono create distinctive, professional typography.

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design patterns and guidelines for you to implement. The AI must write the actual code.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only documentation and prompts. It does not access files, network, or system resources.

faq.question: How is this different from default AI behavior?
faq.answer: Claude defaults to safe, common patterns. This skill provides specific alternatives to create distinctive, branded interfaces.

faq.question: What animation library does this support?
faq.answer: Both CSS transitions and Framer Motion patterns are covered. CSS is preferred for simple interactions; Framer Motion for orchestrated animations.
